qa_pairs.question: What is the name of the directory created by ACPYPE when it finishes processing a molecule, such as `atp.sdf`?
qa_pairs.answer: After processing a file like `atp.sdf`, ACPYPE creates a directory named `atp.acpype` to store all generated output files.

qa_pairs.question: What types of topology and parameter files does ACPYPE generate for various simulation packages when processing a molecule?
qa_pairs.answer: When processing a molecule, ACPYPE generates a variety of topology and parameter files for different simulation packages. These include files for AMBER (`atp_AC.prmtop`, `atp_AC.inpcrd`), GROMACS (`atp_GMX.top`, `atp_GMX.gro`, `atp_GMX.itp`), CHARMM (`atp_CHARMM.rtf`, `atp_CHARMM.prm`), and CNS/XPLOR (`atp_CNS.top`, `atp_CNS.par`). It also produces other relevant files such as `atp_NEW.pdb`, `atp_bcc_gaff2.mol2`, and GROMACS run parameter files (`em.mdp`, `md.mdp`).
